hawkish_phrases: raise the target range for the federal funds rate to 1-1/4 to 1-1/2 percent
the stance of monetary policy remains accommodative, thereby supporting strong labor market conditions
labor market had continued to strengthen and that economic activity had been rising at a solid rate
continued low interest rates risked financial instability in the future, or that the labor market was increasingly tight
expects that economic conditions will evolve in a manner that will warrant gradual increases in the federal funds rate
dovish_phrases: inflation on a 12-month basis is expected to remain somewhat below 2 percent in the near term
both overall inflation and inflation for items other than food and energy have declined this year and are running below 2 percent
several participants observed that survey-based measures of inflation expectations or market-based measures of inflation compensation remained low
some participants expressed concern that persistently weak inflation may have led to a decline in longer-term inflation expectations
Two members preferred to leave the target range at 1 to 1-1/4 percent
summary: The FOMC raised the target range for the federal funds rate by 25bp to 1.25-1.50% with two dissents (Evans and Kashkari preferring to hold), citing continued strong labor market conditions and solid economic growth while explicitly committing to gradual further increases despite inflation remaining below 2%.
